summary: Spanish artist, co-founder of Cubism with Georges Braque. Known for Guernica, Les Demoiselles d'Avignon, Blue Period (The Old Guitarist), Rose Period (Family of Saltimbanques), Las Meninas variations, The Weeping Woman (Dora Maar), Three Musicians, Still Life with Chair Caning. Bull's Head sculpture. Chicago sculpture.

works.name: Guernica (1937)
works.description: Anti-war mural depicting bombing of Basque town. Screaming woman holds dead baby below a bull; flower grows by the hand of a dead man with a sword. Photographed by Dora Maar. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Anti-war mural of bombed Basque town; screaming woman with dead baby below bull; flower by dead man's hand holding sword; documented by Dora Maar
examples: A work by this artist depicts a flower growing by the hand of a dead man who still holds a sword, as well as a screaming woman holding a dead baby below a bull
This artist's progress on a large mural was documented in photos by his muse Dora Maar

works.name: Les Demoiselles d'Avignon (1907)
works.description: Five nude prostitutes, some with faces resembling African masks. Painted at Le Bateau-Lavoir in Montmartre. Inspired by visit to African exhibit at Palais du Trocadero. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Five nude prostitutes, some with faces resembling African masks; painted at Le Bateau-Lavoir in Montmartre; inspired by African exhibit at Palais du Trocadero
examples: five nude prostitutes, some of which have faces resembling African masks
inspired by a visit to an African exhibit at the Palais du Trocadero

works.name: The Old Guitarist (c. 1903-04)
works.description: Blue Period work. Blind, aged street musician sits cross-legged. Style informed by El Greco. Infrared reveals earlier image of a mother and child. Created in response to suicide of Carles Casagemas. Inspired Hockney's The Blue Guitar etchings. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Blue Period; blind aged street musician sits cross-legged, leaning against wall; El Greco-influenced elongation; infrared revealed mother/child underneath
examples: El Greco's style informed the elongated appearance of this painting's title person, who sits cross-legged
Infrared and X-ray imaging of this painting's right side uncovered a cow or bull licking the head of a calf

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Co-founded Cubism with Georges Braque (analytic and synthetic phases). Blue Period, Rose Period. Lovers/muses: Fernande Olivier (Head of a Woman), Dora Maar (The Weeping Woman), Marie-Therese Walter (Le Reve). Sculptures: Bull's Head (bicycle seat + handlebars), Guitar (cardboard/sheet metal), Chicago sculpture (Daley Plaza), absinthe glasses. Three Musicians (Fontainebleau). Still Life with Chair Caning (first collage, oilcloth with chair-caning pattern). Ma Jolie (portrait of Marcelle Humbert). Portrait of Gertrude Stein (80 sittings; face from Iberian sculpture). Women of Algiers (15 versions A-O). Las Meninas variations (58 paintings). Family of Saltimbanques. Massacre in Korea. Designed costumes for Ballets Russes (Satie's Parade). Poetry collection The Burial of the Count of Orgaz. Doves as peace symbol.

clues.clue: Co-founded Cubism with Georges Braque; analytic and synthetic phases; styles became indistinguishable; would not sign paintings
examples: Separate analytic and synthetic phases characterizes the cubist work of this artist who often worked with George Braque

clues.clue: Still Life with Chair Caning: first intentional collage; oilcloth with chair-caning pattern on oval canvas; framed with rope; JOU letters
examples: He glued a piece of oilcloth onto a canvas to create another artwork, which is framed by a piece of actual rope

clues.clue: Portrait of Gertrude Stein: 80-90 sittings; face modeled on Iberian sculpture; 'she will' come to look like it
examples: This portrait, whose face was executed from memory after its artist returned from a trip to look at Iberian sculpture

clues.clue: The Weeping Woman: portrait of lover Dora Maar with face partially obscured by jagged blue-white handkerchief
examples: a woman's face partially obscured by a jagged blue and white handkerchief

clues.clue: Le Reve: portrait of Marie-Therese Walter; Steve Wynn accidentally put elbow through it
examples: Shortly after Steve Wynn bought this painting, he destroyed $50 million of its value by accidentally sticking his elbow through the canvas

clues.clue: Bull's Head: bicycle seat and handlebars welded together
examples: This artist welded together a bicycle seat and handlebars to make his sculpture Bull's Head

clues.clue: Chicago sculpture in Daley Plaza (50-foot-tall, flat oblong face, inspired by Afghan hound)
examples: People like to climb on a large sculpture by this artist in Daley Plaza, Chicago
inspired by an Afghan hound to create a 50-foot-tall sculpture

clues.clue: Las Meninas variations: 58 paintings including 9 of doves; held in Barcelona museum
examples: 58 variations on this painting
this series, which includes nine paintings of doves, is held in its entirety in a museum in Barcelona

clues.clue: Women of Algiers: 15 versions A-O; Version O sold for record $179M; after Matisse's death
examples: A rendition of this painting sold for a record-breaking 179 million dollars

clues.clue: Three Musicians: harlequin, Pierrot, and monk at Fontainebleau
examples: this artist depicted a harlequin, a Pierrot and a monk in Three Musicians

clues.clue: Doves as peace symbol: 1949 World Peace Conference in Paris; Matisse gift; often with flowers
examples: Yellow, red, green, and blue flowers are drawn all over a penciled-in rendering of these animals, which were used as a symbol for the 1949 World Peace Conference

clues.clue: Family of Saltimbanques: Rose Period; woman in red tutu apart from circus performers; inspired Rilke's 5th Duino Elegy
examples: A woman wearing a red tutu sits apart from other circus performers
The fifth Duino Elegy was inspired by this artist's painting The Family of Saltimbanques

clues.clue: Designed costumes for Ballets Russes: Satie's Parade (boxy building costumes), Le Train Bleu curtain
examples: He designed costumes for Satie's Parade which forced dancers to wear boxy costumes representing buildings

clues.clue: Massacre in Korea: firing squad at nude children and women, borrowing from Goya's Third of May
examples: He borrowed the construction of Goya's Third of May for a painting of women standing before a heavily-armored firing squad in North Korea

clues.clue: Banquet for Henri Rousseau (1908) at Bateau-Lavoir: drunken chaos, Andre Salmon ate a hat, sarcastic banner 'Glory To Rousseau'
examples: Andre Salmon chewed on some soap in order to fake an epileptic seizure, antics that got him locked up in a coat room where he ate a hat

comprehensive_summary:
Pablo Picasso was a Spanish artist who co-founded Cubism with Georges Braque, going through analytic and synthetic phases. He is most commonly identified by Guernica and Les Demoiselles d'Avignon.

Guernica (1937) is an anti-war mural depicting the bombing of a Basque town, featuring a screaming woman with a dead baby, a bull, and a flower by a dead man's sword. Dora Maar documented its creation. Les Demoiselles d'Avignon shows five nude prostitutes with faces resembling African masks, painted at Le Bateau-Lavoir and inspired by an African exhibit at the Palais du Trocadero.

The Old Guitarist is a Blue Period work of a blind musician informed by El Greco. Still Life with Chair Caning was the first intentional collage. Portrait of Gertrude Stein required 80-90 sittings and its face was modeled on Iberian sculpture. The Weeping Woman depicts lover Dora Maar. Le Reve depicts Marie-Therese Walter (Steve Wynn put his elbow through it).

His sculptures include Bull's Head (bicycle parts), Guitar (sheet metal), and the Chicago sculpture in Daley Plaza (inspired by an Afghan hound). He created 58 variations of Las Meninas, 15 versions of Women of Algiers (Version O sold for $179M), Three Musicians, Family of Saltimbanques (inspired Rilke's 5th Duino Elegy), and Massacre in Korea. He designed costumes for the Ballets Russes and used doves as peace symbols for the 1949 World Peace Conference.
